clusters.title: Trump arrives in China for high-stakes summit
clusters.neutral_headline: Trump arrives in China for high-stakes summit
bullet_summary: President Trump arrived in Beijing for a critical summit where he said he will push for China's leader to "open up" China to American businesses
Reported by NBC World, CBS News, BBC World and others

items.title: Trump arrives in China for high-stakes summit
items.standfirst: President Trump arrived in Beijing for a critical summit where he said he will push for China's leader to "open up" China to American businesses. NBC Nightly News anchor Tom Llamas reports.
items.content: President Trump arrived in Beijing for a critical summit where he said he will push for China's leader to "open up" China to American businesses. NBC Nightly News anchor Tom Llamas reports.

items.title: How improved U.S.-China relationship could benefit the world
items.standfirst: President Trump arrived in Beijing on Wednesday morning ahead of a high-stakes meeting with Chinese President Xi Jinping. CBS News' Anna Coren and Natalie Brand report.
items.content: President Trump arrived in Beijing on Wednesday morning ahead of a high-stakes meeting with Chinese President Xi Jinping. CBS News' Anna Coren and Natalie Brand report.

items.title: Trade, Iran and Taiwan on the agenda as Trump arrives in China for high-stakes talks with Xi
items.standfirst: The US president says his first request to Xi would be to "open up" China, while Beijing is expected to press Trump on the status of Taiwan.
items.content: The US president says his first request to Xi would be to "open up" China, while Beijing is expected to press Trump on the status of Taiwan.

items.title: Trump arrives in China for historic trip
items.standfirst: US President Donald #Trump has arrived in Beijing for highly anticipated talks with his Chinese counterpart #Xi Jinping. The two leaders are expected to discuss the #Iran war, trade and #US arms sales to #Taiwan. The visit to #China is the first from a US leader since 2017.
items.content: US President Donald #Trump has arrived in Beijing for highly anticipated talks with his Chinese counterpart #Xi Jinping. The two leaders are expected to discuss the #Iran war, trade and #US arms sales to #Taiwan. The visit to #China is the first from a US leader since 2017.

items.title: Trump demands Xi ‘open’ China to US business
items.standfirst: US president arrives in Beijing for two-day summit clouded by geopolitical tensions and conflict over trade and tech
items.content: US president arrives in Beijing for two-day summit clouded by geopolitical tensions and conflict over trade and tech

items.title: Trade, Taiwan and Iran cast shadows on Trump’s China summit with Xi
items.standfirst: President Donald Trump will begin a two-day summit with Chinese President Xi Jinping in Beijing on Thursday as the two countries engage in a fierce trade war and the U.S. conflict with Iran rages on.
items.content: President Donald Trump will begin a two-day summit with Chinese President Xi Jinping in Beijing on Thursday as the two countries engage in a fierce trade war and the U.S. conflict with Iran rages on.

clusters.title: Lockdown in Philippine Senate after gunshots fired
clusters.neutral_headline: Lockdown in Philippine Senate after gunshots fired
bullet_summary: Reported by BBC World, France24, Sky News World and others

items.title: Lockdown in Philippine Senate after gunshots fired
items.standfirst: Police commandos entered the building where a senator wanted by the International Criminal Court had sought refuge.
items.content: Police commandos entered the building where a senator wanted by the International Criminal Court had sought refuge.

items.title: Gunshots fired in standoff at Philippine Senate over ICC suspect
items.standfirst: Gunshots broke out at the Philippine Senate on Wednesday and people ran for cover after a top politician wanted by the International Criminal Court said his arrest was imminent and security forces entered the building. Senator Ronald ​dela Rosa, a former ‌police chief who was the main enforcer of ex-Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte's bloody "war on drugs", had earlier urged people to mobilise to prevent his ⁠arrest and handover to the ICC. FRANCE 24's International Affairs Editor Kethevane Gorjestani tells us more.
items.content: Gunshots broke out at the Philippine Senate on Wednesday and people ran for cover after a top politician wanted by the International Criminal Court said his arrest was imminent and security forces entered the building. Senator Ronald ​dela Rosa, a former ‌police chief who was the main enforcer of ex-Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte's bloody "war on drugs", had earlier urged people to mobilise to prevent his ⁠arrest and handover to the ICC. FRANCE 24's International Affairs Editor Kethevane Gorjestani tells us more.

items.title: Shots fired in Philippine senate where senator accused of crimes against humanity was hiding
items.standfirst: Gunfire broke out in the Philippine senate where a senator is holed up trying to avoid arrest over claims of crimes against humanity.
items.content: Gunfire broke out in the Philippine senate where a senator is holed up trying to avoid arrest over claims of crimes against humanity.

items.title: Gunshots fired in Philippines senate in standoff with senator wanted by ICC
items.standfirst: Ronald dela Rosa, accused of crimes against humanity by international criminal court, has holed up in building to evade arrestGunshots have been fired in the Philippines senate as a senator who is wanted by the international criminal court (ICC) remained holed up in the building to evade arrest.Ronald dela Rosa, a Philippine senator accused of crimes against humanity for his role in overseeing the former president Rodrigo Duterte’s “war on drugs”, has spent two nights in the country’s senate in a standoff with the authorities. clusters.title: Alex Murdaugh's lawyers say they "look forward to a new trial"
clusters.neutral_headline: South Carolina court tosses murder conviction in Alex Murdaugh case
bullet_summary: Alex Murdaugh — the disgraced former lawyer serving a life term for the murders of his wife and son — will get a new trial in South Carolina, the state Supreme Court said on Wednesday
Reported by CBS News, Al Jazeera, BBC World and others

items.title: Alex Murdaugh's lawyers say they "look forward to a new trial"
items.standfirst: Alex Murdaugh's double murder trial drew national attention, but three years later, South Carolina's Supreme Court tossed aside his conviction. Mark Strassmann reports on the new developments.
items.content: Alex Murdaugh's double murder trial drew national attention, but three years later, South Carolina's Supreme Court tossed aside his conviction. Mark Strassmann reports on the new developments.

items.title: Why Alex Murdaugh's murder conviction was overturned
items.standfirst: Three years after Alex Murdaugh was convicted of killing his wife and son, the South Carolina Supreme Court on Wednesday overturned the verdict and granted Murdaugh a new trial. CBS News Katrina Kaufman reports.
items.content: Three years after Alex Murdaugh was convicted of killing his wife and son, the South Carolina Supreme Court on Wednesday overturned the verdict and granted Murdaugh a new trial. CBS News Katrina Kaufman reports.

items.title: South Carolina court tosses murder conviction in Alex Murdaugh case
items.standfirst: The state's high court ruled that Murdaugh was denied a fair trial due to comments from a court clerk suggesting guilt.
items.content: The state's high court ruled that Murdaugh was denied a fair trial due to comments from a court clerk suggesting guilt.

items.title: Court overturns Alex Murdaugh's murder convictions and orders new trial
items.standfirst: The court has ordered a new trial over the June 2021 killings of Paul and Maggie Murdaugh.
items.content: The court has ordered a new trial over the June 2021 killings of Paul and Maggie Murdaugh.

items.title: Alex Murdaugh will get a new murder trial. Here's a timeline of his case
items.standfirst: Alex Murdaugh — the disgraced former lawyer serving a life term for the murders of his wife and son — will get a new trial in South Carolina, the state Supreme Court said on Wednesday.
items.content: Alex Murdaugh — the disgraced former lawyer serving a life term for the murders of his wife and son — will get a new trial in South Carolina, the state Supreme Court said on Wednesday.(Image credit: Gavin McIntyre)

items.title: Netanyahu says he 'secretly visited' UAE during war with Iran
items.standfirst: The UAE denied reports of the "alleged visit" and insisted relations between the two countries were not based on "unofficial arrangements".
items.content: The UAE denied reports of the "alleged visit" and insisted relations between the two countries were not based on "unofficial arrangements".

items.content: The Gulf country says its ties with Israel 'are not based on secrecy or clandestine arrangements'.

items.standfirst: Emirates’ foreign ministry rejects claims that Netanyahu visited the country describing them as ‘baseless’Benjamin Netanyahu has claimed he made a secret trip to the United Arab Emirates at the height of the Iran war to meet the president, Sheikh Mohamed bin Zayed Al Nahyan.“This visit has led to a historic breakthrough in relations between Israel and the UAE,” the Israeli prime minister’s office said on Wednesday night. clusters.title: Senate confirms Trump pick Kevin Warsh as Federal Reserve chair
clusters.neutral_headline: Kevin Warsh confirmed as new US Federal Reserve chair amid controversy
bullet_summary: The US Senate on Wednesday confirmed Kevin Warsh – Trump's top pick – as the new Federal Reserve chair in the most partisan vote ever for the role
Reported by France24, Guardian Business, NPR News and others

items.title: Senate confirms Trump pick Kevin Warsh as Federal Reserve chair
items.standfirst: The US Senate on Wednesday confirmed Kevin Warsh – Trump's top pick – as the new Federal Reserve chair in the most partisan vote ever for the role. Warsh takes over as policymakers warn persistent price pressures could complicate President Donald Trump’s push for lower borrowing costs.
items.content: The US Senate on Wednesday confirmed Kevin Warsh – Trump's top pick – as the new Federal Reserve chair in the most partisan vote ever for the role. Warsh takes over as policymakers warn persistent price pressures could complicate President Donald Trump’s push for lower borrowing costs.

items.title: US Senate confirms Kevin Warsh as Federal Reserve chair, replacing Jerome Powell
items.standfirst: Warsh will serve four-year term as chair, taking over amid rising inflation and pressure from Trump to lower ratesThe US Senate confirmed Kevin Warsh as chair of the Federal Reserve, one of the most powerful roles in the federal government that holds enormous sway over the economy.The 54-45 Senate vote on Wednesday was split along party lines, with the exception of the Democratic senator John Fetterman from Pennsylvania, who joined the Republican majority. It was most divisive confirmation vote for the position in history. Continue reading...
items.content: Warsh will serve four-year term as chair, taking over amid rising inflation and pressure from Trump to lower ratesThe US Senate confirmed Kevin Warsh as chair of the Federal Reserve, one of the most powerful roles in the federal government that holds enormous sway over the economy.The 54-45 Senate vote on Wednesday was split along party lines, with the exception of the Democratic senator John Fetterman from Pennsylvania, who joined the Republican majority. It was most divisive confirmation vote for the position in history. Continue reading...

items.title: Senate confirms Kevin Warsh as next chair of the Federal Reserve
items.standfirst: Warsh has argued there's room for the central bank to lower interest rates, but that could be challenging at a time of rising inflation.
items.content: Warsh has argued there's room for the central bank to lower interest rates, but that could be challenging at a time of rising inflation. (Image credit: Andrew Harnik)

items.title: Kevin Warsh confirmed as new US Federal Reserve chair amid controversy
items.standfirst: Senate confirms Trump pick Kevin Warsh as Federal Reserve chair amid growing concerns over independence.
items.content: Senate confirms Trump pick Kevin Warsh as Federal Reserve chair amid growing concerns over independence.

clusters.title: Russia unleashes massive drone barrage on Ukraine killing six
clusters.neutral_headline: Russia unleashes massive drone barrage on Ukraine killing six
bullet_summary: Ukraine President Volodymyr Zelensky said more than 800 drones were launched, accusing Russia of timing the attack to coincide with US President Donald Trump’s visit to China
Reported by Al Jazeera, France24, BBC World and others

items.title: Russia unleashes massive drone barrage on Ukraine killing six
items.standfirst: Russia has launched a massive daytime drone barrage across Ukraine, killing at least six people.
items.content: Russia has launched a massive daytime drone barrage across Ukraine, killing at least six people.

items.title: Russia unleashes deadly daylight drone blitz across Ukraine
items.standfirst: A sweeping daytime Russian drone assault killed at least six people and wounded dozens across Ukraine on Wednesday, marking a shift in Moscow’s bombardment tactics. Ukraine President Volodymyr Zelensky said more than 800 drones were launched, accusing Russia of timing the attack to coincide with US President Donald Trump’s visit to China.
items.content: A sweeping daytime Russian drone assault killed at least six people and wounded dozens across Ukraine on Wednesday, marking a shift in Moscow’s bombardment tactics. Ukraine President Volodymyr Zelensky said more than 800 drones were launched, accusing Russia of timing the attack to coincide with US President Donald Trump’s visit to China.

items.title: Deadly Russian drone attacks on Ukraine resume after ceasefire expires
items.standfirst: Six people have been killed after Zelensky warned of "more waves" of Russian strikes through Wednesday.
items.content: Six people have been killed after Zelensky warned of "more waves" of Russian strikes through Wednesday.

items.title: Russia targets Ukraine with more than 800 drones in deadly daytime assault
items.standfirst: Strikes killed at least six people as Moscow and Kyiv trade long-range attacks after brief ceasefireEurope live – latest updatesRussia targeted Ukraine with more than 800 drones in a large-scale daytime assault that killed at least six people on Wednesday, hours after a previous deadly barrage.The strikes came as Kyiv and Moscow traded long-range attacks after a brief ceasefire, and despite the latest suggestion from Donald Trump that the war could soon come to an end. Continue reading...
items.content: Strikes killed at least six people as Moscow and Kyiv trade long-range attacks after brief ceasefireEurope live – latest updatesRussia targeted Ukraine with more than 800 drones in a large-scale daytime assault that killed at least six people on Wednesday, hours after a previous deadly barrage.The strikes came as Kyiv and Moscow traded long-range attacks after a brief ceasefire, and despite the latest suggestion from Donald Trump that the war could soon come to an end. Continue reading...

clusters.title: Keir Starmer sets out changes to education, health and courts in king’s speech
clusters.neutral_headline: Keir Starmer sets out changes to education, health and courts in king’s speech
bullet_summary: Reported by Guardian World, France24, NBC World

items.title: Keir Starmer sets out changes to education, health and courts in king’s speech
items.standfirst: King Charles unveils government agenda for the next year as PM faces leadership threat from within LabourKeir Starmer attempted to reassert his authority over his restive party on Wednesday, announcing his plans for the next parliamentary session even as speculation grew that he would be challenged for his job as soon as Thursday.Starmer announced his second king’s speech as prime minister, promising a package of measures with bills to abolish NHS England, overhaul the provision of special educational needs teaching, limit trials by jury, introduce digital ID and end the leasehold system in England and Wales. Continue reading...
items.content: King Charles unveils government agenda for the next year as PM faces leadership threat from within LabourKeir Starmer attempted to reassert his authority over his restive party on Wednesday, announcing his plans for the next parliamentary session even as speculation grew that he would be challenged for his job as soon as Thursday.Starmer announced his second king’s speech as prime minister, promising a package of measures with bills to abolish NHS England, overhaul the provision of special educational needs teaching, limit trials by jury, introduce digital ID and end the leasehold system in England and Wales. Continue reading...

items.title: UK PM Starmer clings on as leadership talk overshadows king's speech
items.standfirst: The pomp of a speech by King Charles III to Britain's parliament Wednesday failed to quell speculation that a senior government minister is poised to launch a leadership challenge against embattled Prime Minister Keir Starmer. The monarch's address opening a new parliamentary session was overshadowed by reports that allies of Health Secretary Wes Streeting had said he was preparing to resign to trigger a contest to oust Starmer from office.
items.content: The pomp of a speech by King Charles III to Britain's parliament Wednesday failed to quell speculation that a senior government minister is poised to launch a leadership challenge against embattled Prime Minister Keir Starmer. The monarch's address opening a new parliamentary session was overshadowed by reports that allies of Health Secretary Wes Streeting had said he was preparing to resign to trigger a contest to oust Starmer from office.

items.title: King Charles enters Britain’s political drama as PM Starmer faces down challengers
items.standfirst: King Charles delivered the King’s Speech as part of the State Opening of Parliament as Westminster is rife with rumor over the prime minister’s future.
items.content: King Charles delivered the King’s Speech as part of the State Opening of Parliament as Westminster is rife with rumor over the prime minister’s future.

clusters.title: Body of second U.S. soldier who went missing in Morocco recovered
clusters.neutral_headline: Body of second U.S. soldier who went missing in Morocco recovered
bullet_summary: The soldier was identified as Spc Mariyah Symone Collington of Taveres, Florida, the US Army Europe and Africa said in a statement
Reported by CBS News, Guardian World

items.title: Body of second U.S. soldier who went missing in Morocco recovered
items.standfirst: The Army identified the soldier as Spc. Mariyah Collington.
items.content: The Army identified the soldier as Spc. Mariyah Collington.

items.title: Remains of second US soldier who went missing during military exercises in Morocco have been recovered
items.standfirst: Mariyah Symone Collington and Kendrick Lamont Key Jr, who also died, had fallen off a cliff during an off-duty hikeThe remains of the second US army soldier who went missing during military exercises in Morocco have been recovered, the army said on Wednesday, ending a multinational search operation that deployed air, naval and artificial intelligence assets.The soldier was identified as Spc Mariyah Symone Collington of Taveres, Florida, the US Army Europe and Africa said in a statement. She was 19 years old. Continue reading...
items.content: Mariyah Symone Collington and Kendrick Lamont Key Jr, who also died, had fallen off a cliff during an off-duty hikeThe remains of the second US army soldier who went missing during military exercises in Morocco have been recovered, the army said on Wednesday, ending a multinational search operation that deployed air, naval and artificial intelligence assets.The soldier was identified as Spc Mariyah Symone Collington of Taveres, Florida, the US Army Europe and Africa said in a statement. She was 19 years old. Continue reading...

items.title: New video shows dramatic Air Force rescue after plane crash off Florida coast
items.standfirst: The U.S. military rescued a small plane that crashed off Florida's East Coast on Tuesday. The passengers were stranded at sea for hours. Cristian Benavides reports.
items.content: The U.S. military rescued a small plane that crashed off Florida's East Coast on Tuesday. The passengers were stranded at sea for hours. Cristian Benavides reports.

items.title: New video shows incredible rescue of plane crash survivors
items.standfirst: Video released by the U.S. military shows how 11 Bahamians whose plane crashed in the Atlantic ocean were rescued by U.S. Air Force personnel. The survivors had been floating on a raft for hours when their rescuers hoisted them to safety. NBC News’ Jesse Kirsch reports.
items.content: Video released by the U.S. military shows how 11 Bahamians whose plane crashed in the Atlantic ocean were rescued by U.S. Air Force personnel. The survivors had been floating on a raft for hours when their rescuers hoisted them to safety. NBC News’ Jesse Kirsch reports.

items.title: Japanese snack packages turning black-and-white as Iran war depletes ink supply
items.standfirst: The packaging on some snacks is turning black-and-white, as the war in Iran disrupts the supply of an ingredient used in colored ink. Calbee's chips originally came in a bright-orange bag.
items.content: The packaging on some snacks is turning black-and-white, as the war in Iran disrupts the supply of an ingredient used in colored ink. Calbee's chips originally came in a bright-orange bag.(Image credit: AP)

items.title: Iran war oil shortage forces Japan snack giant to use black-and-white packaging
items.standfirst: Calbee to switch its brightly coloured packaging to black and white because war has disrupted supply of certain raw materials used in inkJapan’s biggest snack maker has been forced to use black-and-white packaging for some flagship products because of ink ingredient shortages caused by the strait of Hormuz blockade.Calbee, whose potato chip brands in particular are known for brightly coloured bag designs, said 14 of its products would switch to monochrome branding by the end of May. Continue reading...
